news.title: Rajkot civic body crosses 50% of year’s property tax collection target
news.para: Rajkot Municipal Corporation has reached 50% of its annual property tax target, collecting Rs 301.03 crore. Incentives include rebates for early payment and additional discounts for online transactions. The RMC is also actively sealing properties of defaulters and utilizing WhatsApp for efficient bill delivery and payment.

news.title: Centre orders probe into PMAY housing project for CRZ violations in Kharghar
news.para: The Union environment ministry has directed Maharashtra to investigate alleged Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) violations in a Navi Mumbai PMAY housing project. Environmental groups complain the construction is too close to mangroves and the high-tide line, posing climate risks. The ministry asked the state coastal authority to examine the matter and take action if violations are confirmed.

news.title: Major relief for Delhi Development Authority in housing project case
news.para: Delhi's Real Estate Appellate Tribunal (REAT) overturned a Rs 1 crore penalty against the Delhi Development Authority (DDA) for failing to register a housing project. REAT ruled that the Rohini project was completed before RERA's establishment in 2017, making registration unnecessary. The tribunal sent the case back to RERA for a review of the complaint.

news.title: Large construction sites in Gurugram must register on dust portal: DC
news.para: Gurugram administration mandates online registration for construction sites over 500 sqm to monitor dust during GRAP restrictions. A complete ban on waste burning is enforced, with strict guidelines issued for dust control, vehicle checks, and industrial unit monitoring. The Deputy Commissioner stressed collective effort for public health and environmental protection, urging citizen participation.

news.title: Redevelopment rush reaches Malabar Hill in Mumbai, ultra-luxe towers change horizon
news.para: Malabar Hill, Mumbai's prestigious address, is witnessing a redevelopment surge as developers offer lucrative deals to housing societies. New skyscrapers are replacing colonial bungalows, raising concerns about the area's carrying capacity and environmental impact. Despite resident worries, developers highlight the opportunity to upgrade infrastructure and create sustainable homes.

news.title: DDA launches second phase of Jan Sadharan Awaas Yojana housing scheme
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority has rolled out the second phase of its Jan Sadharan Awaas Yojana housing initiative, offering around 1,500 budget-friendly flats across well-connected regions of Delhi. Designed with low-income and economically disadvantaged families in mind, the scheme has sparked significant enthusiasm, with many units snapped up on the very first day.

news.title: SC sets aside Bombay HC ruling & state’s move to classify numerous plots as ‘private forests’
news.para: The Supreme Court overturned a Bombay High Court ruling that allowed the Maharashtra government to classify private lands as "private forests," hindering development. The SC criticized the HC for ignoring binding precedents and procedural requirements, stating the judiciary's strength lies in discipline, not dominion. This decision allows over 14,000 hectares of land to re-enter the buildable pool.

news.title: IndiQube Spaces' posts loss of ₹30 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: IndiQube Spaces Ltd announced a net loss of Rs 29.86 crore for the second quarter of this fiscal year. This figure is notably better than the Rs 52.52 crore loss recorded in the equivalent quarter last year. The company’s income surged impressively, reaching Rs 366.59 crore.

news.title: Vadodara civic body announces interest waiver on overdue property tax till March 31
news.para: The Vadodara Municipal Corporation (VMC) has launched a property tax rebate scheme, waiving interest on overdue payments until March 31 next year. This initiative offers a full interest waiver for properties under the old rent-based system and significant rebates for those under the area-based system. Notice and warrant fees will also be waived.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA razes illegal walls, houses, GMC reclaims ₹30 crore Vasundhara land
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority (GDA) demolished an illegal township spanning 23 hectares in Bhikhanpur and Morta, where developers had constructed roads and boundary walls. This action comes as illegal colonies in the region have increased to 351, occupying 2,944 acres.

news.title: Anant Raj's profit up 31% to ₹138 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a remarkable financial update, Anant Raj Ltd has seen its consolidated net profit soar by 31% to reach Rs 138.18 crore for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. Furthermore, the company's total income climbed to Rs 640.89 crore.

news.title: Prayagraj: PDA announces sale of plots, flats through online registration, auction
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority (PDA) is launching the sale of 206 residential plots across six schemes, with sizes ranging from 60 to 150 square metres. Additionally, 661 flats are also on sale under 11 housing schemes. Registration for both plot and flat sales has commenced, offering affordable housing options and promoting planned urban development.

news.title: Pioneer Urban Land buys 9,419 sq ft flat in DLF's Camellias for ₹95 crore
news.para: Pioneer Urban Land and Infrastructure Ltd made headlines with its latest acquisition: a lavish 9,419 sq ft residence within DLF’s exclusive The Camellias project in Gurugram, valued at an impressive Rs 95 crore. Registered on September 29, this transaction underscores the furious demand for luxury homes among the ultra-wealthy.

news.title: Mohali industrial plot price plunges from ₹8.5 crore to ₹4.5 crore in four months
news.para: Industrial land prices in Mohali's Phase 8-B have significantly dropped, with a 500 sq yard plot selling for Rs 4.5 crore in a recent PSIEC auction, nearly half of its June price. This decline highlights shifting buyer sentiment and increased land availability, though participation remained steady. The auction offered 26 plots, with most settling around Rs 5 crore.

news.title: Pune: PMC launches amnesty scheme for tax defaulters despite opposition from some residents
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation will launch a new property tax amnesty scheme offering a 75% waiver on penalties from November 15, 2025, to January 15, 2026. This comes despite opposition from citizens' groups citing past ineffectiveness and revenue loss.  Over Rs 13,000 crore in property tax remains unrecovered, with penalties significantly exceeding original dues.

news.title: South Goa collector orders mandatory tenant verification
news.para: South Goa landlords and accommodation providers must now verify tenant identities using government-issued IDs and report details to the police. This directive, effective November 5, aims to enhance security across the district. Banks are also mandated to install CCTV and panic buttons at ATMs, with violations facing penalties.

news.title: Tidco seeks Tamil Nadu government's nod to start Global City
news.para: Tidco is seeking administrative sanction to acquire 1,945.19 acres in Maduranthakam taluk for a 2,000-acre global city project near Chennai. The ambitious development will feature IT parks, residential areas, and advanced infrastructure, with Phase I commencing soon. The project aims to integrate AI-driven smart city management and sustainable green-blue infrastructure.

news.title: Pune: PMC issues guidelines for old properties’ safe demolition ahead of redevelopment
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation mandates developers to inform fire brigade and neighbors before demolishing old structures. Neighboring properties must be vacated if at risk of damage. Structural engineer approval is now compulsory, and debris transport requires green cloth covers. These measures aim to prevent past mishaps and pollution during redevelopment.

news.title: About 25 acres of Aravali forest cleared of farmhouses, walls
news.para: Forest officials in Gurugram cleared approximately 25 acres of encroached land in the Raisina Hills of Sohna on Friday. This demolition drive targeted illegal farmhouses and structures built on protected Aravali land, aiming to restore ecological integrity. Further actions are planned following public notices.

news.title: YEIDA gives one year relief to plot allottees, launches new settlement policy
news.para: YEIDA has granted a one-year extension until December 31, 2026, for residential plot construction, benefiting allottees facing delays due to pending infrastructure work. This decision supports thousands of plot holders across five sectors ahead of the Noida International Airport's launch. The authority also approved a new one-time settlement policy and the design for its new headquarters.
